title: Fabric and Apparel Patternmakers
description: Draw and construct sets of precision master fabric patterns or layouts. May also mark and cut fabrics and apparel.

tasks.text: Create a master pattern for each size within a range of garment sizes, using charts, drafting instruments, computers, or grading devices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Input specifications into computers to assist with pattern design and pattern cutting.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Draw details on outlined parts to indicate where parts are to be joined, as well as the positions of pleats, pockets, buttonholes, and other features, using computers or drafting instruments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Make adjustments to patterns after fittings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Compute dimensions of patterns according to sizes, considering stretching of material.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mark samples and finished patterns with information, such as garment size, section, style, identification, and sewing instructions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Draw outlines of pattern parts by adapting or copying existing patterns, or by drafting new patterns.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Test patterns by making and fitting sample garments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Position and cut out master or sample patterns, using scissors and knives, or print out copies of patterns, using computers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Create a paper pattern from which to mass-produce a design concept.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Discuss design specifications with designers, and convert their original models of garments into patterns of separate parts that can be laid out on a length of fabric.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Examine sketches, sample articles, and design specifications to determine quantities, shapes, and sizes of pattern parts, and to determine the amount of material or fabric required to make a product.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Determine the best layout of pattern pieces to minimize waste of material, and mark fabric accordingly.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Create design specifications to provide instructions on garment sewing and assembly.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Trace outlines of paper onto cardboard patterns, and cut patterns into parts to make templates.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Trace outlines of specified patterns onto material, and cut fabric, using scissors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
